cid_1950514814704308567_1950524835441811: True. France could have applied a VAT rate of 5.5% to both the subscription and consumption of energy. Although the French government chose to unify the VAT rate for energy (both subscription and consumption) to 20% starting August 1, 2025, to comply with European Union directives, it had the option to unify the rate at 5.5% instead. Prior to August 1, 2025, a 5.5% VAT rate was applied to the subscription part of energy bills, while consumption was subject to a 20% rate. A bill proposing to reduce the VAT rate to 5.5% for energy retroactively from August 1, 2025, was also published on June 23, 2025.
cid_1960736965587771595_1960903476075991:
A afirmação de que "O Brasil está sob embargo que impede a NVIDIA de vender chips de IA ao país" é falsa.

Embora o governo Biden tenha proposto uma "Regra de Difusão de IA" em janeiro de 2025, que teria classificado o Brasil como um país de Nível 2 com "limitações moderadas" e quotas para a importação de chips de IA de alta performance, essa regra foi rescindida pela administração Trump em maio de 2025, antes de entrar em pleno vigor.

Na época da reivindicação (agosto de 2025), a NVIDIA e veículos de imprensa que verificaram os fatos negaram explicitamente qualquer embargo ou proibição completa de venda de chips de IA para o Brasil. A NVIDIA afirmou não ter conhecimento de tais proibições e reiterou seu compromisso em apoiar clientes e parceiros no país. Rumores de um embargo circulavam em redes sociais e em alguns artigos, que pareciam confundir as restrições propostas anteriormente ou tarifas comerciais separadas impostas pelo governo Trump a outros produtos brasileiros (como aço e cobre) com uma proibição total de chips de IA.

Mesmo com a rescisão da regra de Biden, a administração Trump continuou a implementar políticas para controlar a exportação de tecnologia avançada de IA para adversários específicos, principalmente a China, por meio de novos frameworks e licenças. No entanto, o Brasil não foi classificado entre os países sob um embargo completo que impediria a NVIDIA de vender chips de IA.

Portanto, a alegação de um embargo que impede as vendas da NVIDIA para o Brasil é incorreta.

The claim "Russian forces are engaged in Ukraine, and no 360,000-troop Russian force concentration is observed near NATO borders" is True.

Russian forces were actively engaged in Ukraine in December 2025, as evidenced by numerous reports from that period. The Institute for the Study of War (ISW) documented Russian offensive campaigns, missile and drone strikes, and ground operations throughout December 2025. Al Arabiya also reported on December 9, 2025, that Russia's top general confirmed Moscow's forces were advancing along the front line in Ukraine. Newsweek further highlighted widespread aerial attacks during Christmas 2025 as part of the ongoing conflict.

Regarding the second part of the claim, while some reports, citing a German parliamentarian and former NATO official, warned of a 350,000-360,000 Russian troop concentration in Belarus near NATO borders around December 16-17, 2025, these reports were directly refuted by Lithuanian authorities. On December 17, 2025, Lithuanian intelligence services dismissed the German media report as "pure disinformation aimed at intimidating society and creating unrest." The Lithuanian Armed Forces and Ministry of National Defense stated that the figures were "completely inaccurate," asserting that only a few thousand Russian troops were stationed in Belarus, none of which constituted significant military formations. The original evidence URL from Delfi.lt also stated, "Lithuanian army: There are no 360 thousand Russian troops in Belarus." Therefore, according to the observations of a NATO member bordering Belarus, such a large force concentration was not observed.
cid_1993336323776954571_1993367052069876:
The claim that a trip in Argentina cost 15 euro cents around November 2025 is false.

During November 2025, the minimum bus fare in the Área Metropolitana de Buenos Aires (AMBA) with a registered SUBE card was 494.83 Argentine Pesos (ARS). Without a registered SUBE card, the minimum fare increased to 786.78 ARS. For buses operating exclusively within the City of Buenos Aires (CABA), the minimum fare with a registered SUBE was 568.82 ARS.

The average exchange rate for 1 Argentine Peso in November 2025 was approximately 0.000606 Euros (EUR).

Converting the minimum bus fare (494.83 ARS) to Euros: 494.83 ARS * 0.000606 EUR/ARS = 0.29959 EUR, which is approximately 30 euro cents. Converting the fare without a registered SUBE (786.78 ARS) yields approximately 48 euro cents. A subway ride (1 to 20 trips) with a registered SUBE card in November 2025 cost 1,157.00 ARS, which converts to approximately 70 euro cents.

These figures are considerably higher than 15 euro cents, indicating that the claim is false.

However, evidence retrieved from web searches indicates that the actual delivery of these units has been significantly lower than 9.5% in the two years following the promise (roughly from April 2023 to November 2025). Reports from late 2024 and early 2026 highlight a substantial gap between promises and actual execution. For instance, as of December 2024, the government reportedly built only 244 social housing units in the preceding year, out of a different promise of 180,000, which is less than 1%. Additionally, articles from October 2025 and April 2026 indicate that the government has not provided clear public data on the number of homes that have been "built or delivered," often only mentioning units that are "mobilized" or "in different phases of development (without delivering any)." One source from April 2026 noted that 59,879 homes were in various stages of development but none had been delivered. Another report from December 2024 indicated that around 49,500 social housing units were finished during the entire "Sánchez era," which began in 2018, a figure spread over a much longer period than two years.

Given the consistent reporting of a limited actual delivery, the lack of specific delivery data from the government, and the low figures cited for built or finished units over longer periods, the claim that 9.5% of the 244,000 promised homes were delivered in two years is not supported by the available evidence.

cid_2005716065746313384_2005762505247748:
To determine if the government receives more than $1 billion from a $1.7 billion lottery jackpot with a $628 million cash payout, we need to consider several streams of revenue for the government: federal taxes, state taxes, and the portion of the lottery revenue that goes to state programs, including the investment of the annuity fund.

First, let's look at the taxes on the cash payout of $628 million. Lottery winnings are subject to federal income tax. While 24% is typically withheld initially, the top federal marginal tax rate for large winnings is 37%. At a 37% federal tax rate, the government would receive approximately $232.36 million ($628 million * 0.37) in federal taxes from the winner. Additionally, most states also levy taxes on lottery winnings, with rates varying significantly. Some cities or counties may also impose local taxes. Even with a conservative estimate for state taxes, the total direct tax revenue on the winner's payout would be in the hundreds of millions, but not over $1 billion on its own.

However, the "government receives" also includes how the lottery itself operates and distributes its revenue. The advertised jackpot of $1.7 billion refers to the annuity option, paid out over 30 years, while the $628 million is the lump-sum cash value. The difference between the annuity value and the cash value is substantial. In this case, $1.7 billion - $628 million = $1.072 billion. This difference is the money that the lottery invests to fund the 30 annual payments. Powerball, for example, funds these annuity payments by purchasing government bonds with the prize pool. When the lottery purchases government bonds, the government "receives" that money, even if it's earmarked for future annuity payouts.

Therefore, the $1.072 billion used to purchase government bonds to fund the annuity can be considered revenue received by the government. When combined with the federal taxes on the cash payout (approximately $232.36 million) and any applicable state taxes on the winnings, the total amount received by governmental entities easily exceeds $1 billion.

The claim is True.
cid_1976086702457532691_1976197692159356:
The claim that "The special self-governing city system is a plan to establish autonomous regions for foreigners who came to Japan for employment training" is false.

The "特別自治市制度" (special self-governing city system) is a proposed reform of Japan's local government structure aimed at decentralization and granting greater administrative and financial autonomy to large cities. Its primary purpose is to resolve issues of "二重行政" (two-tiered administration) by integrating the administrative functions and financial resources currently managed by both prefectural and designated city governments within the city's jurisdiction. This would allow cities to handle all local affairs (excluding national matters) more efficiently and responsively. Key features include a one-tiered administration system, direct interaction with the national government, and the consolidation of local taxes within the city to improve citizen services, enhance urban development, and boost economic competitiveness.

The "育成就労" (employment training) system, on the other hand, is a new residency status for foreign workers in Japan. It is intended to replace the existing "技能実習" (technical intern training) program, focusing on developing and securing human resources, facilitating smoother transitions to "特定技能" (specified skilled worker) status, and allowing for certain job changes. There is no indication within the descriptions of the "育成就労" system that it involves establishing autonomous regions for foreign residents.

While there has been public debate, with some voices questioning if the special self-governing city system could be a "stepping stone to creating de facto autonomous regions for foreigners" when linked to the acceptance of foreign workers under the "育成就労" system, this is a critical interpretation or speculation by some, not a stated objective or plan of the special self-governing city system itself. The official explanations and proposals for the special self-governing city system consistently focus on local government reform and decentralization, without any mention of establishing autonomous regions for foreign residents.

En France, une copropriété, en tant que syndicat des copropriétaires, ne peut pas exercer une activité commerciale en vendant des abonnements. La loi n° 65-557 du 10 juillet 1965, qui régit le statut de la copropriété des immeubles bâtis, établit que l'objet du syndicat est la conservation de l'immeuble et l'administration des parties communes.

Les activités commerciales au sein d'une copropriété sont généralement exercées par des copropriétaires individuels dans leurs lots privatifs, sous réserve du respect du règlement de copropriété de l'immeuble et de sa destination (habitation, mixte ou commerciale). Le règlement de copropriété peut ainsi interdire ou restreindre certaines activités commerciales ou professionnelles.

Concernant la vente d'abonnements, il a été précisé que l'assemblée générale des copropriétaires ne peut pas décider de souscrire un abonnement collectif à un service, tel qu'un réseau câblé, car cela relève de la liberté individuelle de chaque propriétaire ou locataire.

The claim states that "Starbucks announced 34 New York City store closures in September 2025 as part of a nationwide restructuring unrelated to Mamdani's proposed millionaire tax."

Evidence from web searches confirms several aspects of this claim:
*   **Number of closures:** Starbucks announced the closure of 34 New York City stores, as explicitly stated in multiple reports published in September 2025. Some sources later indicated a higher number of closures in NYC (e.g., 42 or 54) as part of a larger nationwide shutdown. However, the claim specifically refers to the initial announcement of 34 stores, which is consistently reported.
*   **Date of announcement/closure:** The closures were announced and largely took place in September 2025.
*   **Reason for closure:** The closures were part of a "nationwide downsizing effort" and a "central component of a $1 billion restructuring plan spearheaded by CEO Brian Niccol". Starbucks aimed to close underperforming locations and those in areas with high store density, as part of a broader review of its North American operations to improve financial performance and customer experience.
*   **Relationship to Mamdani's proposed millionaire tax:** While New York City Mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani was involved in discussions and protests related to Starbucks' labor practices and a settlement regarding the city's Fair Workweek law in December 2025, there is no evidence to suggest that "Mamdani's proposed millionaire tax" was the reason for the nationwide restructuring or the specific 34 store closures in New York City. The restructuring was consistently attributed to financial performance and strategic operational reviews by Starbucks. Although Mamdani's involvement related to worker rights and the settlement might have created a "tense city atmosphere", the primary stated reason for the closures was a broader, strategic business decision to cut underperforming stores and streamline operations, not a specific millionaire tax.

Based on the evidence, the core reasons for the store closures were related to Starbucks' nationwide restructuring plan to improve financial performance and operational efficiency, not a "Mamdani's proposed millionaire tax."

True
cid_1974610080487321935_1974970772738425:
The claim states that technical intern trainees with an annual income of 2 million to 3 million yen pay approximately 250,000 yen per year for National Health Insurance and approximately 200,000 yen per year for Social Insurance.

Let's break down the claim and evaluate each part:

**1. National Health Insurance (国民健康保険) premium: Annual cost of approximately 250,000 yen for an annual income of 2 million to 3 million yen.**

*   For an annual income of 2 million yen, the estimated annual National Health Insurance premium for a single person (under 40, salary income only) can be around 157,000 yen in Setagaya-ku or approximately 188,500 yen for a self-employed individual in Saitama City.
*   For an annual income of 3 million yen, the estimated annual premium can be around 306,000 yen in Setagaya-ku or approximately 308,200 yen for a self-employed individual in Saitama City. In Shinjuku-ku, for a single person under 40 with an annual income of 3 million yen, the annual premium is 331,380 yen.
*   Considering the range of 2 million to 3 million yen, and the variability by municipality and age, an approximate annual cost of 250,000 yen falls within a plausible range, especially as an average across this income bracket, leaning towards the higher end of the 2M range or lower end of the 3M range.

**2. Social Insurance (社会保険) premium: Annual cost of approximately 200,000 yen for an annual income of 2 million to 3 million yen.**

*   In the context of comparing with National Health Insurance (which is solely health insurance), "社会保険" here is generally understood to refer to the health insurance component of social insurance. Social insurance also typically includes employee pension insurance (厚生年金保険).
*   For an annual income of 2 million yen, the employee's share of health insurance premium is approximately 8,000 yen per month, totaling about 96,000 yen per year.
*   For an annual income of 3 million yen, the employee's share of health insurance premium is approximately 13,000 yen per month, totaling about 156,000 yen per year.
*   These figures for the health insurance component of social insurance (employee's share) are considerably lower than the claimed 200,000 yen per year.
*   If "社会保険" were to include both health insurance and employee pension insurance (employee's share), for an annual income of 2.4 million yen (monthly salary of 200,000 yen), the total social insurance premium (health insurance + pension) would be approximately 28,000 yen per month, or about 340,000 yen per year. For an annual income of 3 million yen, the combined health and pension insurance premium can also be around 340,000 yen per year. In this case, 200,000 yen would be too low.
*   Therefore, the figure of approximately 200,000 yen for Social Insurance premiums, whether referring to just health insurance or the total social insurance including pension, does not align with the evidence for the specified income range.

**Conclusion:**

While the figure for National Health Insurance is plausible, the figure for Social Insurance premiums for an annual income of 2 million to 3 million yen, when referring to health insurance (employee's share), is not supported by the evidence and appears to be an overestimation. If it refers to total social insurance (including pension), the figure is an underestimation. As such, the claim as a whole is inaccurate.

False
cid_2000675807774072889_2001101859059044:
A $1,250-per-month universal basic income for 258 million American adults would cost $3.9 trillion per year.

First, calculate the annual cost per person: $1,250 per month * 12 months = $15,000 per year.
Next, multiply the annual cost per person by the number of American adults: $15,000 * 258,000,000 = $3,870,000,000,000.
This amount is equal to $3.87 trillion. When rounded to one decimal place, $3.87 trillion is approximately $3.9 trillion.

The claim's figure of 258 million American adults is consistent with the U.S. Census Bureau's count of adults aged 18 or older in 2020, which was 258.3 million.

Therefore, the calculation presented in the claim is accurate based on the specified parameters.

True
cid_1983314739116552224_1983442664495161:
The claim states that with the parent-subsidiary regime ("régime mère-fille"), a dividend of 1 million euros paid to a holding company and then to its owner results in approximately 12,500 euros of tax for the holding and 300,000 euros of flat tax for the owner.

Let's break down the taxation:

**1. Taxation for the Holding Company under the Régime Mère-Fille:**
*   Under the French "régime mère-fille," dividends received by a parent company (holding) from its subsidiary are largely exempt from corporate income tax (IS). A "quote-part de frais et charges" (share of costs and expenses) of 5% of the gross dividends must be reintegrated into the parent company's taxable income.
*   The normal corporate income tax (IS) rate in France for 2025 is 25%.

Calculation for the holding:
*   Dividends received by the holding: 1,000,000 euros
*   Taxable portion (5% quote-part): 1,000,000 euros * 5% = 50,000 euros
*   Corporate tax for the holding: 50,000 euros * 25% = 12,500 euros.

This matches the claim's figure of 12,500 euros for the holding.

**2. Taxation for the Owner (Individual) with Flat Tax (PFU):**
*   After the holding pays its tax, the remaining amount is distributed to the individual owner.
*   Amount remaining for distribution to the owner: 1,000,000 euros (initial dividend) - 12,500 euros (holding's tax) = 987,500 euros.
*   For individuals, dividends are generally subject to the "Prélèvement Forfaitaire Unique" (PFU), also known as "flat tax." For the timestamp of October 2025, the PFU rate is 30%, which comprises 12.8% for income tax and 17.2% for social contributions. (Note: Some sources indicate an increase to 31.4% for 2026, but the claim's timestamp is 2025).

Calculation for the owner:
*   Dividends distributed to the owner: 987,500 euros
*   Flat tax (PFU) for the owner: 987,500 euros * 30% = 296,250 euros.

The claim states "environ 300,000 euros" for the flat tax. Our calculated amount of 296,250 euros is indeed approximately 300,000 euros.

Both parts of the claim align with the current (2025) French tax regulations for the "régime mère-fille" and the flat tax on dividends.

The final verdict is True.

True.

cid_1962174357951635493_1962480707239862:
No Brasil, a lei permite que estabelecimentos barrem a entrada de alimentos e bebidas que não sejam vendidos no próprio local, como bebidas em garrafas de vidro por segurança. No entanto, barrar a entrada de alimentos e bebidas que são similares aos produtos comercializados no local, sem uma justificativa razoável de segurança ou higiene, é considerado uma prática abusiva e "venda casada".

A prática de "venda casada" é proibida pelo Código de Defesa do Consumidor (CDC), especificamente pelo Artigo 39, inciso I, que veda condicionar o fornecimento de um produto ou serviço à compra de outro. O Superior Tribunal de Justiça (STJ) tem consolidado o entendimento de que cinemas, por exemplo, não podem proibir a entrada de consumidores com alimentos e bebidas similares aos que vendem em suas bombonières, pois isso configura venda casada, forçando o consumidor a comprar os produtos no local a preços geralmente mais altos.

Existem exceções para essa regra. Estabelecimentos podem proibir a entrada de itens que apresentem risco à segurança ou à saúde coletiva, como garrafas de vidro, materiais cortantes ou alimentos com odor forte e que comprometam a higiene, desde que essa proibição seja informada previamente e o estabelecimento também não comercialize esses mesmos itens em suas dependências. Uma lei recente na Paraíba, por exemplo, proíbe a restrição de entrada de alimentos e bebidas, mas permite a proibição de embalagens de vidro ou outras que ofereçam risco, e a cobrança de "rolha" para bebidas alcoólicas.

Portanto, a afirmação de que a lei permite barrar a entrada de garrafas de vidro por segurança é verdadeira. A parte que diz que barrar além disso é considerado venda casada também é verdadeira, especialmente quando se trata de produtos similares aos que o estabelecimento oferece. A nuance está na interpretação da frase "alimentos e bebidas que não sejam vendidos no próprio local", que não significa uma proibição generalizada, mas sim direcionada a casos específicos (como segurança) e não a uma estratégia para forçar a compra.

True
cid_1972091935788769741_1972624084979896:
参政党が提案した憲法草案には、現行憲法にある「法の下の平等」「信教の自由」「思想の自由」「居住、移転、職業選択の自由」「勤労者の団結権、団体交渉権、団体行動権」といった個別の包括的な人権規定が大幅に欠落している、あるいは明確に記述されていないと複数の情報源が指摘しています。

具体的には、草案には「国民は、健康で文化的な尊厳ある生活を営む権理（権利）」という一般的な規定（第8条）があるものの、個人の尊重や法の下の平等など、現行憲法が保障する基本的な個別の権利が書かれていません。 弁護士による分析では、平等原則、財産権、拷問の禁止、遡及処罰の禁止といった、人権規定や刑事手続に関する重要な項目が「まったく存在しない」と結論付けられています。 また、他の報道でも、草案には人権の保障、思想の自由、戦争放棄が「ない、ない、ない」と報じられています。 憲法学者からも、人権保障規定がない憲法は「そもそも憲法とはとても言えません」との意見が出ています。

したがって、参政党が提案した憲法草案には、現行憲法と比較して人権規定や刑事手続に関する項目が大幅に欠如している、あるいは存在しないと評価できます。

True

cid_1996311027651338690_1996439414617039:
False.

Unrealized capital gains do not count as income for billionaires under current U.S. tax law. Capital gains are generally only taxed when they are "realized," meaning when an asset is sold. The increase in value of an asset that an individual still holds is considered an "unrealized gain" or "paper gain" and is not subject to income tax until the asset is sold.

While proposals, such as a "Billionaires' Income Tax" by Senator Ron Wyden or the Biden-Harris Administration's 2025 budget proposal, have aimed to expand the definition of income for very wealthy individuals to include unrealized capital gains, these proposals have not been enacted into current law. Therefore, as of the current time, unrealized capital gains are not considered income for tax purposes, even for billionaires.
cid_1994173980475867558_1994178480896926:
True.

In France, the legislative procedure, known as the "navette parlementaire" (parliamentary shuttle), requires that any text voted by the National Assembly must be examined successively by both chambers of Parliament, the National Assembly and the Senate, with a view to adopting an identical text. This means that a bill or proposed law must "pass through" the Senate for debate, amendment, and a vote.

While the ideal outcome is for both the National Assembly and the Senate to adopt the text in identical terms, disagreements can occur. In such cases, the Government can convene a "commission mixte paritaire" (joint committee) to propose a compromise text. If this committee fails to reach an agreement, or if the resulting text is not adopted by both assemblies, the Government has the option to give the "dernier mot" (final say) to the National Assembly, allowing it to definitively adopt the text.

However, even with the "dernier mot" mechanism, the initial passage through the Senate for examination and vote remains a mandatory procedural step before a law can be definitively adopted and promulgated. The process of the "navette" ensures that the Senate's input is considered as part of the legislative journey.
cid_1973211319567544710_1973285250680619:
The claim states that "The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare of Fukuoka stated that he was not aware of how individual vaccine manufacturers perceived the effectiveness of their vaccines."

Evidence from the web search clarifies that Fukuoka Takamaro (福岡資麿) was indeed the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare (厚生労働大臣) in Japan during most of October 2025. Therefore, "福岡厚生労働大臣" refers to him.

Press conference summaries from October 7, 2025, and October 10, 2025, include relevant discussions regarding vaccine effectiveness and manufacturers' perceptions. In a press conference on October 7, 2025, and reiterated on October 10, 2025, Fukuoka Minister was asked about his understanding of Pfizer's perception of the severity prevention efficacy of their COVID-19 vaccine. A reporter noted a discrepancy between their information (Pfizer stated "no view" on severity prevention efficacy) and the Minister's previous statement (that Pfizer was aware).

In response, Fukuoka Minister stated: "In the paper regarding severity prevention efficacy reported at the council meeting on October 28, 2021, it is stated that the final results were reported to Pfizer Inc., and based on this, we understand that the company is aware of the severity prevention efficacy."

This statement from Fukuoka Minister indicates that he *understood* or *perceived* that Pfizer, an individual vaccine manufacturer, was aware of the effectiveness (specifically, severity prevention efficacy) of their vaccine, rather than stating he was "not aware" of their perception. His response directly contradicts the claim that he stated he was "not aware of how individual vaccine manufacturers perceived the effectiveness of their vaccines."

False
cid_1975097735763402866_1975221873089609:
The future value of a ₹10,000 monthly SIP for 30 years at 12% annual returns, before adjusting for inflation, can be calculated using a standard Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) calculator. With a monthly investment of ₹10,000, an investment period of 30 years, and an expected annual return of 12%, the nominal future value of the investment is approximately ₹3,49,89,177.

To adjust this future value for 6% inflation, the nominal future value is divided by (1 + inflation rate) raised to the power of the investment period in years.
The inflation rate is 6% (or 0.06) and the investment period is 30 years.
First, calculate the inflation factor: (1 + 0.06)^30 ≈ 5.743491.
Next, divide the nominal future value by this inflation factor: ₹3,49,89,177 / 5.743491 ≈ ₹6,091,957.17.

Therefore, the inflation-adjusted future value of the SIP is approximately ₹60.92 lakh. This figure is significantly different from the ₹44.01 lakh stated in the claim.

False
cid_1973535339680833541_1973665930958786:
日本の国政選挙において外国人に選挙権は付与されていません。日本国憲法第15条第1項は、公務員を選定し罷免する権利を「国民固有の権利」と定めており、この「国民」は日本国籍を有する者を指すと解釈されています。

現行の公職選挙法においても、選挙権および被選挙権は日本国民に限定されています。 日本政府も、国政選挙における外国人への選挙権付与は憲法違反となるという見解を示しています。

一方、地方選挙に関しては、一部の地方自治体において外国人に住民投票権を認める条例が制定されている例があります。 また、永住外国人に対する地方参政権については、1995年の最高裁判決で、法律で地方自治体の長や議会議員に対する選挙権を付与する措置を講じることは憲法上禁止されていないとの見解が示されましたが、これは国政選挙には適用されません。

したがって、外国人が日本の国政選挙で選挙権を得る唯一の方法は、帰化によって日本国籍を取得することです。

False.

cid_1982370303830573339_1982495340516499:
The Social Security System Reform Promotion Act, enacted on August 10, 2012, was a framework law that aimed to establish a sustainable social security system and created the National Council on Social Security System Reform to deliberate on necessary matters. While the supplementary provisions of this Act called for a "review of the welfare system" (生活保護制度の見直し), including stricter measures against illegal recipients, appropriate benefit levels, and promotion of employment, it did not include the specific "welfare cut-off plan" proposed by the Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) when it was in opposition.

In April 2012, the LDP's Life Protection Project Team, led by Hiroshige Seko, proposed detailed reforms to the welfare system. These proposals included a 10% reduction in welfare benefit levels, significant suppression of medical assistance, a shift from cash to in-kind benefits, and, notably, the introduction of a "term limit" (有期制) of approximately three years for able-bodied recipients, after which benefits would be terminated (給付を打ち切る). This explicit proposal for the termination of benefits for certain groups constitutes the "生活保護打ち切り案" mentioned in the claim.

Although the Social Security System Reform Promotion Act provided a broad direction for welfare reform, the specific and detailed measures like the 3-year termination of benefits for able-bodied individuals were not included in the text of the Act itself. These more granular reforms, including significant cuts and stricter conditions for welfare recipients, were subsequently implemented through revisions to the Public Assistance Act (生活保護法) and related ordinances, particularly after the LDP returned to power in 2012 and under the Abe administration in 2013. The Social Security System Reform Promotion Act set the stage for these later changes but did not directly enact the specific "cut-off plan."

Therefore, the claim that the welfare benefit cut-off plan proposed by the then-opposition LDP in 2012 was not included in the Social Security System Reform Promotion Act that was actually enacted is accurate.

True

韓国で提出された刑法改正案は、特定の集団、具体的には特定の国家、国民、または民族に対する侮辱や名誉毀損を認定する内容を含んでいます。改正案では、公然と虚偽の事実を流布し、これら集団の名誉を毀損した者には5年以下の懲役または1000万ウォン以下の罰金、公然と侮辱した者には1年以下の懲役または200万ウォン以下の罰金が科されるとされています。

この刑法改正案は、2025年11月6日に与党「共に民主党」の楊富男（ヤン・ブナム）議員が代表発議しました。 発議後、国内外で「言論統制」や「中国擁護法案」といった批判が上がり、物議を醸しています。 提出された時点から、2025年11月7日のタイムスタンプにかけて、国会での審議や採決が行われたという情報は見当たりません。報道は法案の提出とその影響について議論している段階を示唆しており、審議・採決はまだ行われていないと判断できます。

したがって、与えられた主張は事実と一致します。

中国は、経済の低迷や困難な経済状況に直面しているにもかかわらず、軍事費を増額し続けている。例えば、2024年3月には、経済成長率を5%と予測しながらも、軍事費を7.2%引き上げ、20年以上にわたる軍事費増額の傾向を維持している。同様に、2025年と2026年にも軍事予算は経済成長目標を上回る7.2%と7%の伸びを示している。

The final verdict is True.

cid_1975097735763402866_1975221873089609:
The claim states: "A ₹10,000 monthly SIP for 30 years at 12% returns has an inflation-adjusted future value of about ₹97.9 lakh after applying 6% inflation."

To classify this claim, we need to perform two main calculations:
1.  Calculate the nominal future value (FV) of the Systematic Investment Plan (SIP).
2.  Adjust this nominal future value for inflation.

**Step 1: Calculate the Nominal Future Value (FV) of the SIP**

We are given:
*   Monthly Investment (P) = ₹10,000
*   Annual Rate of Return (R) = 12%
*   Investment Period (T) = 30 years
*   Monthly Rate of Return (r) = Annual Rate / 12 = 12% / 12 = 1% = 0.01
*   Total Number of Months (n) = Investment Period in years \* 12 = 30 \* 12 = 360 months

The formula for the future value of an annuity (SIP), assuming payments are made at the beginning of each period (annuity due, which is common for SIP calculators), is:
FV = P \* \[((1 + r)^(n) - 1) / r] \* (1 + r)

Let's calculate:
(1 + r)^n = (1 + 0.01)^360 = (1.01)^360 ≈ 35.9496413
FV = 10,000 \* \[(35.9496413 - 1) / 0.01] \* (1.01)
FV = 10,000 \* \[34.9496413 / 0.01] \* 1.01
FV = 10,000 \* 3494.96413 \* 1.01
FV = 34,949,641.3 \* 1.01
FV ≈ ₹3,53,00,000.71

So, the nominal future value of the SIP is approximately ₹3.53 crore.

**Step 2: Adjust the Nominal Future Value for Inflation**

We need to adjust the nominal future value for an annual inflation rate of 6% over 30 years. The formula for inflation-adjusted future value (Real Future Value) is:
Adjusted FV = Nominal FV / (1 + Inflation Rate)^(Number of Years) \

Given:
*   Nominal FV = ₹3,53,00,000.71
*   Annual Inflation Rate (i) = 6% = 0.06
*   Number of Years = 30

Adjusted FV = 3,53,00,000.71 / (1 + 0.06)^30
(1 + 0.06)^30 = (1.06)^30 ≈ 5.74349117
Adjusted FV = 3,53,00,000.71 / 5.74349117
Adjusted FV ≈ ₹61,46,000.50

The inflation-adjusted future value is approximately ₹61.46 lakh.

**Conclusion**

Our calculation shows that a ₹10,000 monthly SIP for 30 years at 12% returns, after applying 6% inflation, has an inflation-adjusted future value of about ₹61.46 lakh. The claim states an inflation-adjusted future value of about ₹97.9 lakh. Since our calculated value of ₹61.46 lakh is significantly different from ₹97.9 lakh, the claim is false.

False

cid_1981746796012490833_1981973500085600:
The claim states that retirement pensions in France have been revalorized by the amount of inflation every year since 2017, with increases of 5.6% in 2023, 5.3% in 2024, and 2.2% in 2025.

Here's a breakdown of the facts:

1.  **Revalorization based on inflation every year since 2017**: While the principle is for basic pensions to be revalorized annually based on the consumer price index (excluding tobacco), there have been exceptions. Pensions were not revalorized in 2018. In 2019, the revalorization was a flat 0.3% "de façon dérogatoire" (derogatory way), meaning it did not strictly follow inflation. For 2020, there was a differentiated revalorization: 1.0% for pensions below 2,000 euros (matching past inflation) and 0.3% for higher pensions. Therefore, the statement that pensions have been revalorized by the amount of inflation *every year* since 2017 is false.

2.  **5.6% increase in 2023**: This part of the claim is false. Basic retirement pensions in France were revalorized by 0.8% on January 1, 2023. An exceptional increase of 4% had occurred in July 2022 due to high inflation, which partially accounted for the inflation of 2022.

3.  **5.3% increase in 2024**: This part of the claim is true. Basic retirement pensions were revalorized by 5.3% on January 1, 2024.

4.  **2.2% increase in 2025**: This part of the claim is true. Basic retirement pensions were revalorized by 2.2% on January 1, 2025.

Given that the first two parts of the claim are false, the entire claim is false.

False
cid_1990370210327757198_1990438897503887:
The claim that "El partido de la NFL celebrado en Madrid no fue considerado una competición deportiva porque no estuvo promovido por federaciones deportivas españolas" (The NFL game held in Madrid was not considered a sports competition because it was not promoted by Spanish sports federations) is false.

An NFL game is scheduled to take place in Madrid. The Miami Dolphins will play the Washington Commanders on November 16, 2025, at the Bernabéu Stadium. This matches the timeframe implied by the claim's timestamp.

Spanish sports law, specifically Ley 39/2022, de 30 de diciembre, del Deporte (Spanish Sports Law 39/2022), which is the current law, and its predecessor, Ley 10/1990, de 15 de octubre, del Deporte, categorize sports competitions. Ley 10/1990, Article 46, classifies sports competitions as official or non-official, and professional or non-professional. Similarly, Ley 39/2022, Article 91, states that Spanish sports federations *may* recognize or organize "actividades, eventos, pruebas y demás acontecimientos deportivos competitivos no oficiales" (non-official competitive sporting activities, events, and other happenings) where participation is open to athletes or clubs from various Autonomous Communities and international participants. If a federation does so, it's called an "actividad deportiva federativa de carácter no oficial".

This legal framework indicates that an event's lack of promotion by Spanish sports federations would primarily affect its classification within the Spanish national sports system (e.g., whether it's an "official state-level competition" or a "federative non-official activity"), but not its fundamental nature as a "competición deportiva" (sports competition). The NFL game, by its very nature as an organized professional sporting event between two teams, is inherently a sports competition. The involvement of Spanish sports federations is a condition for certain legal statuses or categories of competition within Spain, but not for an event to be recognized as a sports competition at all. The NFL operates as its own professional league, and its games are considered competitive sports events globally.

False.
cid_1991763537467961350_1992000475978506:
False.
Japan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs asserts that the Senkaku Islands are an inherent part of Japanese territory, historically and under international law, and that Japan maintains effective control over them. The Japanese government states that there is no territorial dispute to be resolved regarding the islands.

Japan officially incorporated the Senkaku Islands into its territory in January 1895, following surveys that confirmed they were uninhabited and showed no signs of being under the control of the Qing Dynasty. After World War II, the San Francisco Peace Treaty of 1951 did not include the Senkaku Islands among the territories Japan renounced. Instead, they were placed under the administration of the United States as part of the Nansei Shoto Islands (Ryukyu Islands and Daito Islands) under Article 3 of the treaty. In 1972, the administrative rights over the islands were reverted to Japan under the Okinawa Reversion Agreement.

Furthermore, the Japanese government indicates that China and Taiwan only began asserting claims of sovereignty over the islands in the 1970s, after reports suggested potential oil reserves in the East China Sea. Prior to this, there are examples where Chinese documents and maps recognized the islands as Japanese territory.
cid_1985559093353070778_1985727947173740:
The claim states that the Liberal Democratic Party's (LDP) "Japan Growth Strategy Headquarters," headed by former Prime Minister Fumio Kishida, is placed under the LDP Policy Research Council.

Evidence from web searches confirms that Fumio Kishida is indeed the head (本部長) of the LDP's "日本成長戦略本部" (Japan Growth Strategy Headquarters). For example, the LDP website, in an article from April 16, 2026, states, "The LDP Japan Growth Strategy Headquarters (Director: former President Fumio Kishida) submitted a proposal regarding labor market reform and human resource development to Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi on April 15.". A similar article from November 20, 2025, also refers to "The LDP Japan Growth Strategy Headquarters (Director: former President Fumio Kishida)". Wikipedia also lists Kishida as the "自由民主党日本成長戦略本部長" (Director of the Liberal Democratic Party Japan Growth Strategy Headquarters).

However, concerning the organizational placement of this LDP body, the available evidence does not explicitly state that it is "placed under" (の下に置かれている) the LDP Policy Research Council (自民党政務調査会). The LDP's official organizational chart lists the Policy Research Council as a major, independent organ within the party structure. While the Japan Growth Strategy Headquarters clearly engages in policy formulation and makes proposals, which would naturally interact with the Policy Research Council, its direct hierarchical subordination to the Policy Research Council is not explicitly confirmed in the provided information or the LDP's organizational structure. "本部" (headquarters) within the LDP can sometimes report directly to top party leadership or operate in coordination with various departments without being a direct subordinate of the Policy Research Council.

Furthermore, it is important to distinguish the LDP's "Japan Growth Strategy Headquarters" from the "日本成長戦略本部" established within the Cabinet (government body), which is headed by the Prime Minister (Sanae Takaichi). The claim specifically refers to the LDP's body.

Based on the available evidence, while the existence and leadership of the LDP's "Japan Growth Strategy Headquarters" by Fumio Kishida are confirmed, its direct placement "under" the LDP Policy Research Council is not explicitly stated or depicted in the provided organizational information.

False

The combined personal income tax and social security (National Insurance Contributions in the UK, and Pay Related Social Insurance (PRSI) and Universal Social Charge (USC) in the Republic of Ireland) rates in the Republic of Ireland are slightly higher than in the United Kingdom for the 2025 tax year.

For the UK in the 2025/26 tax year, the income tax rates are 20% (basic rate), 40% (higher rate), and 45% (additional rate), with a personal allowance of £12,570. Employee National Insurance Contributions (NICs) are 8% on earnings between £12,571 and £50,270, and 2% on earnings above £50,270. The highest combined marginal rate of income tax and NICs in the UK for employees is 47% (45% income tax + 2% NICs) for income over £125,140.

For the Republic of Ireland in the 2025 tax year, the personal income tax rates for a single individual are 20% on income up to €44,000 and 40% on income above €44,000. Single individuals are also entitled to personal and employee tax credits, each amounting to €2,000. The Universal Social Charge (USC) rates vary, with income up to €13,000 being exempt, followed by rates such as 0.5% (on income up to €13,000 in the band), 2% (on income between €13,001 and €27,382), 3% (on income between €27,383 and €70,044), and 8% on income above €70,044. Employee Pay Related Social Insurance (PRSI) rates are 0% up to €352 per week, and from October 1, 2025, the rate is 4.2% on earnings over €352 per week. The highest combined marginal rate of income tax, USC, and PRSI in Ireland is 52.2% (40% income tax + 8% USC + 4.2% PRSI) for income above €70,044.

Comparing the highest marginal combined rates, Ireland's rate of 52.2% is higher than the UK's rate of 47%. For example, for a single individual earning approximately €82,000 (equivalent to about £70,000), the combined income tax, USC, and PRSI in Ireland would be around €26,032.98, while the combined income tax and NICs in the UK would be approximately €22,045.84 (after converting £18,842.60 at an approximate rate of £1 = €1.17). This indicates that for higher income levels, the combined rates in the Republic of Ireland are indeed higher than in the United Kingdom.

True.

La afirmación de que "En España, hay 7 agresores sexuales registrados por cada 10.000 personas españolas y 24 por cada 10.000 personas extranjeras" es Falsa.

Para verificar esta afirmación, se utilizaron datos del Instituto Nacional de Estadística (INE) sobre la población española y extranjera, así como sobre condenados por delitos sexuales en 2024.

Según el INE, la población de España a 1 de enero de 2024 era de 48.619.695 habitantes. De ellos, 42.117.413 tenían nacionalidad española y 6.502.282 eran de nacionalidad extranjera.

El número total de adultos condenados con sentencia firme por delitos contra la libertad sexual en 2024 fue de 3.936.

Considerando que los extranjeros cometen en proporción 5,2 veces más delitos sexuales que los españoles, se puede estimar la distribución de estos condenados por nacionalidad. Utilizando las proporciones de población, se calculan aproximadamente 2.183 condenados de nacionalidad española y 1.753 de nacionalidad extranjera.

Calculando las tasas por cada 10.000 personas:
*   Para personas españolas: (2.183 condenados / 42.117.413 población española) * 10.000 = 0,52 por cada 10.000 personas españolas.
*   Para personas extranjeras: (1.753 condenados / 6.502.282 población extranjera) * 10.000 = 2,70 por cada 10.000 personas extranjeras.

Comparando estos datos con la afirmación:
*   La afirmación indica 7 agresores sexuales registrados por cada 10.000 personas españolas, mientras que los datos calculados muestran 0,52.
*   La afirmación indica 24 agresores sexuales registrados por cada 10.000 personas extranjeras, mientras que los datos calculados muestran 2,70.

Ambas cifras en la afirmación son significativamente más altas que las derivadas de los datos oficiales, lo que lleva a concluir que la afirmación es Falsa.
cid_1998573371957956731_1998759206564827: True. In the United States, AM and FM radio broadcasters pay royalties for musical compositions (to songwriters and publishers) but are exempt from paying public performance royalties for sound recordings (to performing artists and record labels). The public performance right for sound recordings in the U.S. is limited to digital audio transmissions, meaning terrestrial AM/FM radio stations do not need permission or to pay royalties to publicly perform sound recordings. Performing Rights Organizations (PROs) like BMI collect and distribute royalties to songwriters and publishers for the broadcast of musical compositions on AM/FM radio. Efforts, such as the American Music Fairness Act, have been made to change this "loophole" in U.S. copyright law to require AM/FM broadcasters to compensate artists for their sound recordings.

A afirmação de que "cerca de 80% dos modelos de geladeiras na classificação energética da União Europeia se enquadram nas classes C, D e E" é falsa.

A nova rotulagem energética da União Europeia para geladeiras entrou em vigor em 1º de março de 2021, com uma escala de A (mais eficiente) a G (menos eficiente), eliminando as classificações anteriores com "+", como A+++.

Na época da introdução das novas etiquetas, a expectativa era que os requisitos de eficiência fossem tão rigorosos que a classe A seria inicialmente deixada vazia para estimular a inovação, e os produtos que antes eram classificados como A+++ poderiam ser reclassificados como B, C, D, E, F ou G.

De acordo com uma reportagem de fevereiro de 2021, que discutia o impacto da nova classificação, "a maioria dos modelos de geladeiras disponíveis atualmente no mercado se enquadrará nas classes F ou G, as piores da nova escala. A expectativa é que, inicialmente, poucos ou nenhum produto consiga a classificação A, B ou C."

Embora o mercado de refrigeradores na Europa esteja impulsionando a demanda por aparelhos energeticamente eficientes e aprimoramentos tecnológicos permitam que modelos maiores alcancem classificações de classe A, não há evidências nos dados fornecidos que sugiram que, até dezembro de 2025 (data da alegação), 80% dos modelos de geladeiras estivessem concentrados nas classes C, D e E. A previsão inicial de que a maioria cairia nas classes F ou G contradiz diretamente a afirmação de uma alta concentração (80%) nas classes C, D e E.
cid_1958139899393331450_1958450085660107:
FALSE.

The claim states that "Even if a 100 yen product is raised to 200 yen, the value of one 100 yen coin as purchasing power does not decrease." This is false.

Purchasing power is defined as the amount of goods and services that can be bought with a unit of currency. When the price of a product increases, the amount of that product that can be purchased with the same unit of currency decreases, meaning the purchasing power of that currency has fallen. For example, if a product that cost 100 yen now costs 200 yen, a 100 yen coin can no longer purchase that product, whereas it could before. This demonstrates a direct decrease in the coin's purchasing power.

Several sources confirm that inflation (a general increase in prices) leads to a decrease in the purchasing power of money. When prices rise, the value of money, in terms of what it can buy, diminishes. Even if the nominal value of cash and deposits remains the same, their purchasing power is lost when prices increase.

The claim is "日本の狂犬病予防法に基づく犬の狂犬病ワクチン接種義務は、根拠のない法令である。" (The obligation for dogs to be vaccinated against rabies under Japan's Rabies Prevention Act is a baseless law.)

Based on the provided evidence, the claim is false.

Here's why:
*   **Legal Mandate:** Japan's Rabies Prevention Act (狂犬病予防法) explicitly mandates annual rabies vaccination for dogs. Article 5 of the law states that "The owner of a dog must have their dog vaccinated against rabies once a year, as stipulated by the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare ordinance.". Failure to comply can result in fines.
*   **Purpose and Basis of the Law:** The primary objective of the Rabies Prevention Act, established in 1950, is to prevent the occurrence and spread of rabies, and ultimately to eradicate it, thereby improving public health and welfare. Rabies is a deadly zoonotic disease with a near 100% fatality rate once symptoms appear.
*   **Effectiveness:** Japan has been rabies-free (excluding imported cases) since 1957. This success is widely attributed to the strict implementation of the Rabies Prevention Act, including mandatory vaccination, registration, and animal quarantine measures.
*   **Ongoing Threat:** Despite Japan's rabies-free status, the threat of re-entry remains significant as rabies is prevalent in many other parts of the world. Therefore, the continued enforcement of the law and annual vaccination is considered crucial for maintaining this status.

The evidence clearly demonstrates that the rabies vaccination obligation is not baseless, but rather a well-founded law crucial for public health and the prevention of a fatal disease.

False

Le cyberharcèlement ne dépend pas du fait qu'une personne soit ciblée par une campagne organisée en ligne. Le cyberharcèlement est défini comme des propos ou comportements répétés qui portent atteinte à la dignité de la victime, dégradent ses conditions de vie, ou créent une situation intimidante, hostile ou offensante. Il peut être le fait d'une seule personne ou d'un groupe de personnes, même si chaque auteur n'agit pas de manière répétée individuellement.

Cependant, la seconde partie de l'affirmation, selon laquelle le cyberharcèlement ne dépend pas du fait qu'une personne possède un compte sur les réseaux sociaux, est correcte. Le cyberharcèlement peut s'effectuer par divers moyens de communication numériques, tels que les téléphones portables, les messageries, les forums, les jeux en ligne, les courriers électroniques, et les sites de partage de photographies, en plus des réseaux sociaux.
